Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check where the player is now and whether they've reached or interacted with the target at rows 36-39, cols 32-35.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] in ('N', 'W', 'b'):
        print(f"Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check if any blue targets disappeared
print(f"\nTotal nodes: {len(seg)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

The player (green N) is now at rows 41-43, cols 32-35. The white W is at row 40. The target directly above (blue b at node 8) is at rows 37-38, cols 33-34. The player is close but hasn't reached it yet. Let me move UP once more to get to rows 37-39 area, which should put the player right at/overlapping the target.
Node 4 (blue, pixels=28) is the horizontal bar - it's at rows 28-31, cols 28-39. This is the gray bar inside the dark frame.
Let me move UP once more to try to reach the target at rows 37-38.

<function=python>
<parameter=code>

action(['UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
